title: Sarga 88
summary: Rama's divine birth, childhood, protection of Sage Vishwamitra's rituals from demons, breaking of Shiva's bow, and marriage to Sita. Addresses youth, childhood, education, overcoming early obstacles, and marriage.

sections.summary: This passage begins with “Having heard that story related to Ila, narrated by Rama, Lakshmana and Bharata were greatly astonished.” and develops toward “But Budha, having just seen her, fell under the sway of Cupid's arrows, lost control of himself, and then stirred in the water.”

This passage begins with “As he gazed at Ila, who was more beautiful than anything in the three worlds, a thought came to him: "Who indeed is this superior goddess?"” and develops toward “Then, those many women, having been transformed into Kimpurushis upon hearing the words of Budha, the son of Soma, resided near that mountain.…”

verses.sanskrit: तां कथामिलसम्बद्धां रामेण समुदीरिताम् । लक्ष्मणो भरतश्चैव श्रुत्वा परमविस्मितौ ।। 7.88.1 ।।
verses.translation: Having heard that story related to Ila, narrated by Rama, Lakshmana and Bharata were greatly astonished.

verses.sanskrit: तौ रामं प्राञ्जली भूत्वा तस्य राज्ञो महात्मनः । विस्तरं तस्य भावस्य तदा पप्रच्छतुः पुनः ।। 7.88.2 ।।
verses.translation: The two, with folded hands, then again asked Rama for the detailed account of the great-souled king's condition.

verses.sanskrit: कथं स राजा स्त्रीभूतो वर्तयामास दुर्गतिः । पुरुषः स यदा भूतः कां वृत्तिं वर्तयत्यसौ ।। 7.88.3 ।।
verses.translation: They inquired how that king lived in misfortune after becoming a woman, and what kind of life he led when he became a man again.

verses.sanskrit: तयोस्तद्भाषितं श्रुत्वा कौतूहलसमन्वितम् । कथयामास काकुत्स्थस्तस्य राज्ञो यथागतम् ।। 7.88.4 ।।
verses.translation: Hearing their speech, which was full of curiosity, Rama (Kakutstha) narrated the king's story exactly as it had happened.

verses.sanskrit: तमेव प्रथमं मासं स्त्रीभूता लोकसुन्दरी । ताभिः परिवृता स्त्रीभिर्ये च पूर्वपदानुगाः ।। 7.88.5 ।।
verses.translation: For that very first month, having become a woman of unparalleled beauty, she was surrounded by the women who had previously been part of his retinue.

verses.sanskrit: तत्काननं विगाह्वाशु विजह्रे लोकसुन्दरी । द्रुमगुल्मलताकीर्णं पद्भ्यां पद्मदलेक्षणा ।। 7.88.6 ।।
verses.translation: The lotus-petal-eyed woman, beautiful in the world, quickly entered that forest, which was filled with trees, bushes, and creepers, and roamed through it on foot.

verses.sanskrit: वाहनानि च सर्वाणि सा त्यक्त्वा वै समन्ततः । पर्वताभोगविवरे तस्मिन्रेमे इला तदा ।। 7.88.7 ।।
verses.translation: Then, Ilā, having completely abandoned all her conveyances, delighted in that wide mountain cavity.

verses.sanskrit: अथ तस्मिन्वनोद्देशे पर्वतस्याविदूरतः । सरः सुरुचिरप्रख्यं नानापक्षिगणैर्युतम् ।। 7.88.8 ।।
verses.translation: Then, in that forest region not far from the mountain, there was a lake, very beautiful in appearance and filled with various flocks of birds.

verses.sanskrit: ददर्श सा त्विला तस्मिन्बुधं सोमसुतं तदा । ज्वलन्तं स्वेन वपुषा पूर्णसोममिवोदितम् ।। 7.88.9 ।।
verses.translation: Then, Ilā saw Budha, the son of Soma, in that place, shining with his own body like the risen full moon.

verses.sanskrit: तपन्तं च तपस्तीव्रमम्भोमध्ये दुरासदम् । यशस्करं कामकरं कारुण्ये पर्यवस्थितम् ।। 7.88.10 ।।
verses.translation: He was performing intense and formidable penance in the midst of water, established in compassion, bestowing fame and fulfilling desires.

verses.sanskrit: सा तं जलाशयं सर्वं क्षोभयामास विस्मिता । सहगैः पूर्वपुरुषैः स्त्रीभूतै रघुनन्दन ।। 7.88.11 ।।
verses.translation: O delight of the Raghus, astonished, she agitated that entire body of water along with her companions, who were formerly men but had also become women.

verses.sanskrit: बुधस्तु तां समीक्ष्यैव कामबाणवशं गतः । नोपलेभे तदा ऽ ऽत्मानं सञ्चचाल तदा ऽ ऽम्भसि ।। 7.88.12 ।।
verses.translation: But Budha, having just seen her, fell under the sway of Cupid's arrows, lost control of himself, and then stirred in the water.

verses.sanskrit: इलां निरीक्षमाणस्तु त्रैलोक्याभ्यधिकां शुभाम् । चिन्तां समभ्यतिक्रामत् का न्वियं देवताधिका ।। 7.88.13 ।।
verses.translation: As he gazed at Ila, who was more beautiful than anything in the three worlds, a thought came to him: "Who indeed is this superior goddess?"

verses.sanskrit: न देवीषु न नागीषु नासुरीष्वप्सरःसु च । दृष्टपूर्वा मया काचिद्रूपेणानेन शोभिता ।। 7.88.14 ।।
verses.translation: Never before have I seen anyone adorned with such beauty, neither among goddesses, Nāga women, Asura women, nor Apsaras.

verses.sanskrit: सदृशीयं मम भवेद्यदि नान्यपरिग्रहः । इति बुद्धिं समास्थाय जलात्कूलमुपागमत् ।। 7.88.15 ।।
verses.translation: Having firmly resolved that she would be suitable for him if she were not already taken by another, he then went from the water to the bank.

verses.sanskrit: आश्रमं समुपागम्य ततस्ताः प्रमदोत्तमाः । शब्दापयत धर्मात्मा ताश्चैनं च ववन्दिरे ।। 7.88.16 ।।
verses.translation: Having approached the hermitage, the righteous Budha then called out to those excellent women, and they, in turn, bowed down to him.

verses.sanskrit: स ताः पप्रच्छ धर्मात्मा कस्यैषा लोकसुन्दरी । किमर्थमागता चैव सर्वमाख्यात मा चिरम् ।। 7.88.17 ।।
verses.translation: The righteous king asked them, "Whose is this most beautiful woman, and for what purpose has she come? Tell me everything without delay."

verses.sanskrit: शुभं तु तस्य तद्वाक्यं मधुरं मधुराक्षरम् । श्रुत्वा स्त्रियश्च ताः सर्वा ऊचुर्मधुरया गिरा ।। 7.88.18 ।।
verses.translation: Having heard his auspicious, sweet, and melodious words, all those women replied with a sweet voice.

verses.sanskrit: अस्माकमेषा सुश्रोणी प्रभुत्वे वर्तते सदा । अपतिः काननान्तेषु सहास्माभिश्चरत्यसौ ।। 7.88.19 ।।
verses.translation: This beautiful-hipped woman is always in authority over us; she wanders in the forest regions without a husband, accompanied by us.

verses.sanskrit: तद्वाक्यमव्यक्तपदं तासां स्त्रीणां निशम्य च । विद्यामावर्तनीं पुण्यामावर्तयत स द्विजः ।। 7.88.20 ।।
verses.translation: Having heard that speech with indistinct words from those women, that brahmin recited the sacred spell that causes return.

verses.sanskrit: सो ऽर्थं विदित्वा सकलं तस्य राज्ञो यथागतम् । सर्वा एव स्त्रियस्ताश्च बभाषे मुनिपुङ्गवः ।। 7.88.21 ।।
verses.translation: Having understood the entire situation of that king as it had occurred, the foremost among sages then spoke to all those women.

verses.sanskrit: अत्र किम्पुरुषीर्भूत्वा शैलरोधसि वत्स्यथ । आवासस्तु गिरावस्मिञ्छीघ्रमेव विधीयताम् ।। 7.88.22 ।।
verses.translation: You shall dwell here on the mountain slope, becoming Kimpurushis, and a dwelling place on this mountain should be arranged quickly.

verses.sanskrit: मूलपत्रफलैः सर्वा वर्तयिष्यथ नित्यदा । स्त्रियः किम्पुरुषान्नाम भर्तऽन्समुपलप्स्यथ ।। 7.88.23 ।।
verses.translation: All of you shall always sustain yourselves with roots, leaves, and fruits, and as women, you shall obtain husbands named Kimpurushas.

verses.sanskrit: ताः श्रुत्वा सोमपुत्रस्य वाचं किम्पुरुषीकृताः । उपासाञ्चक्रिरे शैलं वध्वस्ता बहुलास्तदा ।। 7.88.24 ।।
verses.translation: Then, those many women, having been transformed into Kimpurushis upon hearing the words of Budha, the son of Soma, resided near that mountain. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीये आदिकाव्ये श्रीमदुत्तरकाण्डे ऽष्टाशीतितमः सर्गः ।। 88 ।। Thus ends the eighty-eighth sarga of Uttara Kanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.
